deities.name: Reshmit of the Heavy Voice
deities.edicts: Attack at your whim, shatter objects, sing
deities.anathemas: Repair an object you've broken, seek lost memories

deities.name: Reymenda
deities.edicts: Bring definitive ends to known evils, consider genuine requests for aid, sacrifice when others can’t
deities.anathemas: Adopt or have a child, directly undo the death of another or the ending of something, treat children as a burden or a mistake, withhold news of another’s ending

deities.name: Rhan-Tegoth
deities.edicts: Create statues of Rhan-Tegoth, sacrifice living sentient creatures
deities.anathemas: Outer Gods and Great Old Ones

deities.name: Ristrentho
deities.edicts: Clear obstacles to progress, spend part of each day in motion
deities.anathemas: Dam a river or stream, hold a creature against its will

deities.name: Rivethun
deities.edicts: maintain balance and understanding between the mortal and spirit world, internally cultivate yourself, serve your community
deities.anathemas: continually place mortal matters above spiritual ones, mislead a petitioner and fail to set it right, willingly misrepresent the will of spiritual entities

deities.name: Rokoga Gin
deities.edicts: Seek new horizons, protect travelers on the road, learn something new each day
deities.anathemas: Willingly stay in one place for longer than your duty requires, accost travelers

deities.name: Rovagug
deities.edicts: destroy all things, free Rovagug from his prison
deities.anathemas: create something new, let material ties restrain you, torture a victim or otherwise delay its destruction

deities.name: Rowdrosh
deities.edicts: Aid your community, protect those you have authority over, treat animals with respect
deities.anathemas: Abandon your community, neglect those in your care, torment animals

deities.name: Rull
deities.edicts: Create chaos at every opportunity, be loud wherever you go, spread fear and terror in Rull’s name
deities.anathemas: Enforce law and order, show mercy, allow morals to impede the sowing of chaos

deities.name: Sairazul
deities.edicts: Aid childbirths, care for Sairazul’s children, mine responsibly, shelter others within stone and earth
deities.anathemas: Collapse an earthen structure on a creature, damage subterranean natural wonders

deities.name: Saloc
deities.edicts: Help creatures grow and find purpose, offer second chances to failures, study different perspectives on ethics
deities.anathemas: Manipulate or remove a creature’s emotions with magic, spread nihilism or hopelessness

deities.name: Sangpotshi
deities.edicts: live selflessly; heed the wisdom of the learned, the wise, and those in stations of authority
deities.anathemas: needlessly break significant traditions, actively interfere with the perfection efforts of another soul or allow others to do so

deities.name: Sarenrae
deities.edicts: destroy the Spawn of Rovagug, protect allies, provide aid to the sick and wounded, seek and allow redemption
deities.anathemas: create undead, lie, deny a repentant creature an opportunity for redemption, fail to strike down evil

deities.name: Sarshallatu
deities.edicts: feel privileged to witness wonders of creation, find value in being, change to better know yourself
deities.anathemas: change yourself for another, be consumed by temporary things, let others define your soul

deities.name: Scal
deities.edicts: Strive for physical and mental perfection, tap into one’s inner darkness to destroy anything that compromises one’s pursuit of righteous perfection, take vengeance when necessary
deities.anathemas: Indulge in gluttonous or lazy behavior, give up without a fight, refuse a duel

deities.name: Seafarer's Hope
deities.edicts: give thanks for safe passage, respect the sea, view all storms with optimism and perseverance
deities.anathemas: put off travel until there are calm waters, refuse aid to those stranded at sea, stay too long on land

deities.name: Sekhmet
deities.edicts: Slaughter your enemies, drink the blood of defeated foes, heal battle injuries
deities.anathemas: Spare an evil fiend, fail to placate Sekhmet with daily rituals

deities.name: Selket
deities.edicts: Avenge the wronged, protect the dead and the vulnerable, use poison and suffocation, heal others
deities.anathemas: Poison someone you didn’t intend to, harm a creature as punishment for a different creature’s crime, desecrate a corpse

deities.name: Seramaydiel
deities.edicts: Create music in public where strangers can hear and be inspired by you, compose original poetry about that which you are passionate about, support the arts in any way you can
deities.anathemas: Destroy musical instruments, disparage others who create music, encourage others to not support artisans

deities.name: Set
deities.edicts: Bring chaos to society, murder those who stand in your way, defeat your foes with personal strength and cunning, kill fiends
deities.anathemas: Permit the spread of the desert to fertile lands, refuse to fight your own battles, aid Apep or other omnicidal gods

deities.name: Shadow Cabinet
deities.edicts: glean sensitive information through underhanded means, infiltrate powerful organizations, keep your own secrets
deities.anathemas: allow your agenda to be discovered through carelessness, destroy information when it doesn’t personally benefit you

deities.name: Shapes of the Fading Luster
deities.edicts: craft with metal, either allow a piece of metal to rust away or continually keep it polished, learn the highest secrets of alchemy
deities.anathemas: destroy metallic objects quickly instead of letting them rust away, purposefully transform a precious metal into a base metal

deities.name: Shawnari
deities.edicts: Fiercely protect your privacy, rely only on yourself, waste time in frivolous pursuits
deities.anathemas: Keep strict track of your time, reach out to others when you feel alone

deities.name: Shax
deities.edicts: Plot and commit murders, tell lies, torture creatures
deities.anathemas: Sleep in a building with fewer than five rooms, allow a victim to escape due to gloating

deities.name: Shei
deities.edicts: Empower local communities, learn from elders, share and teach from experiences
deities.anathemas: Force others to follow your path, inflict void damage, age others or steal life with magic

deities.name: Shelyn
deities.edicts: be peaceful, choose and perfect an art, lead by example, see the beauty in all things
deities.anathemas: destroy art or allow it to be destroyed, unless saving a life or pursuing greater art; refuse to accept surrender

deities.name: Shivaska
deities.edicts: Accelerate impending doom, demand subservience from your lessers, oppress the weak
deities.anathemas: Allow a prisoner to go free, show mercy without personal benefit

deities.name: Shizuru
deities.edicts: practice your chosen skill every day, honor your ancestors, protect nature and society from corruption and destruction
deities.anathemas: abandon a companion in need, harm the innocent, disrespect your ancestors, parlay with truce breakers, separate lovers, cast an unholy spell

deities.name: Shumunue
deities.edicts: Celebrate births, make time for nature, recreate life in your works
deities.anathemas: Permanently damage a plant or wood creature, polymorph another without permission

deities.name: Shyka
deities.edicts: Learn from the past, leave hourglasses in unusual places, give random gifts, create ephemeral things
deities.anathemas: Willingly tread where time does not pass

deities.name: Sicva
deities.edicts: Spread invasive species to new ecosystems, corrupt good ideals to promote domination and oppression, inspire duty and loyalty through coercion and fear
deities.anathemas: Support a rebellion, give freely expecting nothing in return, aid in ending a plague or infection

deities.name: Sifkesh
deities.edicts: Spread doubt among the faithful, ruin the reputation of religions, provoke wrongdoers to suicide instead of allowing for redemption
deities.anathemas: Spread hope, offer forgiveness, sincerely honor or call upon another god

deities.name: Sigek
deities.edicts: betray your superiors and allies, cause crippling pain in others, host parasites
deities.anathemas: cure parasitic infections in others, maintain an alliance longer than a year, restore a non-nindoru creature to life

deities.name: Sithhud
deities.edicts: Be as inexorable as a glacier, endure the elements, seek vengeance no matter how long it takes
deities.anathemas: Forgive even the smallest slights, offer shelter during a blizzard or ice storm

deities.name: Sivanah
deities.edicts: show the beauty in illusions, pursue the nature of truth, respect the need for secrets
deities.anathemas: let a creature permanently labor under the falsehood of your illusions, reveal a secret you have sworn to keep

deities.name: Skode
deities.edicts: Take the finest or healthiest specimen among any animals you hunt, respect your prey, protect your community, show bravery in the face of danger
deities.anathemas: Waste the animals you hunt, show cruelty to animals, abandon your community in a time of danger

deities.name: Skrymir
deities.edicts: Seek out and explore unfamiliar places, use wit over violence whenever possible, lift spirits with harmless jokes and pranks
deities.anathemas: Settle down in one place for too long, harm someone with your jokes or pranks, turn down the chance to solve a riddle

deities.name: Sky Keepers
deities.edicts: remove anyone that would threaten those who live among the skies, encourage others to find a way to live among the clouds, honor the might of storms
deities.anathemas: interfere with the natural phases of the sky, remove a creature's innate means to fly

deities.name: Smiad
deities.edicts: Slay evil dragons, aid benevolent dragons, train others in dragonslaying techniques
deities.anathemas: Accept the words of an evil dragon as truth, allow benevolent dragons to be harmed by your actions or inaction, fail to teach others the differences between dragons

deities.name: Sobek
deities.edicts: Take what you want, indulge in base desires, feast on luxurious food, kill demons and evil creatures
deities.anathemas: Cower from fights, despoil the land, kill the innocent

deities.name: Soralyon
deities.edicts: Study monuments, craft golems and artwork from stone, guard ancient sites
deities.anathemas: Defile sacred buildings, knowingly unearth evil monuments, destroy historical artifacts

deities.name: Sorrow's Sword
deities.edicts: avenge the wronged, destroy evildoers, lead the charge in battle, remember your community's history
deities.anathemas: cower from combat, forgive those who have committed grave offenses, abandon or deceive allies

deities.name: Sovyrian Conclave
deities.edicts: explore the worlds outside and within, learn and appreciate traditional elven arts, crafts, and magic
deities.anathemas: have an unhealthy obsession or attachment, irreparably damage the natural environment (such as by overhunting or strip mining)

deities.name: Srikalis, Sritaming, and Sribaril
deities.edicts: die in battle, revel in protective violence, support what is yours
deities.anathemas: lay waste to nature, kill indiscriminately without reason or conviction, choose not to raid when it is needed

deities.name: Ssila’meshnik
deities.edicts: Embrace change, pursue freedom, present opposing perspectives, seek out new skills and experiences
deities.anathemas: Adhere to a single fate, honor an oath you are not expected to break, sow compromise among opposing groups

deities.name: Stag Mother of the Forest of Stones
deities.edicts: Act as a responsible steward of your environment, provide shelter and succor for women and children in need
deities.anathemas: Harm a pregnant mother or innocent child, including animals

deities.name: Stone's Blood
deities.edicts: develop skills useful to your community (especially crafting, mining, and trading), honor your ancestors through tradition and rituals
deities.anathemas: dishonor your family, irreparably damage an ancestral relic, willingly break a contract or oath

deities.name: Sturovenen
deities.edicts: Act with conviction and confidence, be a passionate and responsible leader to your people or companions
deities.anathemas: Command a subordinate to perform a task you’re not willing to perform yourself

deities.name: Sun Wukong
deities.edicts: live life freely, drink, play pranks
deities.anathemas: refuse a reasonable bet or duel, let social pressure change your behavior

deities.name: Suyuddha
deities.edicts: Study the art of warfare and strategy, strive to take and maintain control of your situation, hunt and slay hounds of Tindalos
deities.anathemas: Let your emotions dictate your tactics, hunt felines for sport, permit others of your rank or lower to calculate tactics on your behalf

deities.name: Szuriel
deities.edicts: End all mortal life through war, obliterate faith
deities.anathemas: Show mercy to creatures who do not worship Szuriel, choose to marry or have children

deities.name: Talons of the Godclaw
deities.edicts: be an exemplar of divine order and balance, demonstrate the resilience of your faith by shielding others from chaos, instruct others who show promise in the ways of your order
deities.anathemas: profess to understand law or order better than the pantheon, take no action when the presence of chaos has been revealed

deities.name: Tanagaar
deities.edicts: Defend rightful borders, scout or patrol for evil, wait to strike until you have the advantage
deities.anathemas: Abandon your post, tolerate poaching, torment animals

deities.name: Teki Stronggut
deities.edicts: Remember those whom others forget, help those who need it the most, aid other misfits in their goals
deities.anathemas: Give up on your cause, punish someone due to failure or incompetence, abandon a fellow adventurer

deities.name: Telastmar
deities.edicts: Aid natural migrations, chart the stars and tides, observe and calculate patterns, build orreries
deities.anathemas: Use a net, waste a fish, block out the stars with unnatural light

deities.name: Telvrys
deities.edicts: Sow new life in desolate places, foster the growth and well-being of flora, patiently mentor disciples, climb to high places
deities.anathemas: Abandon an endeavor before making a stubborn attempt, harm fragile ecosystems except in the pursuit of saving greater plant life, demean those who attempt difficult tasks

deities.name: Teshallas
deities.edicts: Embrace old age, grow or tend poisonous plants, learn from your experiences, respect the wisdom of the elderly, use poisons
deities.anathemas: Cling to the past, needlessly kill venomous creatures, poison someone you didn’t intend to

deities.name: Thalaphyrr
deities.edicts: Learn from your failures, watch over prisoners
deities.anathemas: Consider anything a poor use of time, allow a prisoner to escape

deities.name: Thamir
deities.edicts: Seize any opportunity that would benefit you, solve your problems with violence, hide your true intentions
deities.anathemas: Steal from the poor, beg for help or mercy from a fellow worshipper of Thamir

deities.name: The Anointing of Kings
deities.edicts: pursue perfection in yourself and others, help others seize their destiny, search for lost rulers
deities.anathemas: refuse a promotion, work against the fulfillment of prophecy, kill the last member of a noble line

deities.name: The Deliberate Journey
deities.edicts: plan for travel and bring what you need, identify the best route, make deliberate decisions for the benefit of all who travel, travel with a purpose
deities.anathemas: fail to prepare for a journey, let chance decide which way to go, put travelers under your care in danger

deities.name: The Devourer
deities.edicts: engage in acts of destruction, kill sentient beings, tear down civilizations
deities.anathemas: repair an object that won’t inflict greater destruction; create new technology, magic, or life without a destructive purpose

deities.name: The Divine Dare
deities.edicts: boast of your daring deeds, participate in competitions, revel in taking risks
deities.anathemas: cheat in an honest competition, let others take credit for your accomplishments, refuse a non-fatal challenge

deities.name: The Endless Road
deities.edicts: see the world to find your place in it, seek common bonds, share community histories
deities.anathemas: close your ears and heart to others, do not seek to better yourself, resist the call to explore new places

deities.name: The Enlightened Scholar's Path
deities.edicts: correct ignorance and counter false knowledge with respect, maintain and extol the value of historical buildings and artifacts, research carefully and accurately, share what you know, teach the truth
deities.anathemas: destroy books or monuments, keep silent when faced with false information, refuse to share knowledge

deities.name: The Freeing Flame
deities.edicts: free others from oppression, release harmful tethers be they physical or spiritual, work toward your best self and inspire others to follow
deities.anathemas: allow tyrants to escape punishment after their reign ends, enslave or oppress others, prevent others from honestly expressing themselves

deities.name: The Godclaw
deities.edicts: eliminate chaos at its root, be an exemplar of order (worthy of not one, but five), seek the universal laws that transcend individual faiths
deities.anathemas: rest when there is lawlessness to fight, believe you know or understand more than the pantheon

deities.name: The Green Mother
deities.edicts: Frolic in vegetation, manipulate people, use what you kill, prey on the weak
deities.anathemas: Hold a secret for too long, discriminate against sex workers or use their trade to harm them

deities.name: The Laborer's Bastion
deities.edicts: encourage others to strive toward their own potential, give your all to work and play, have hope that tomorrow will be a better day, stand up for the oppressed
deities.anathemas: cheat, give into or spread despair, keep silent when faced with oppression, work without respite or enjoyment

deities.name: The Lady of the North Star
deities.edicts: seek lost knowledge, uncover hidden mysteries, honor that which is forgotten, pursue immortality
deities.anathemas: destroy sources of knowledge, make public that which should remain secret, desecrate a memorial, grow complacent about one's ignorance and mortality, commit torture or murder, knowingly harm an innocent

deities.name: The Lantern King
deities.edicts: Play pranks, seek new jokes, leave lit lanterns in unusual places
deities.anathemas: Be completely honest, ruin or explain a good joke

deities.name: The Last Breath
deities.edicts: punish oppressors, resist those who would control you and others, turn others' power against them
deities.anathemas: give in to despair, surrender

deities.name: The Lost Prince
deities.edicts: Aid the depressed, wear somber clothing, maintain neutrality, ruminate on the past
deities.anathemas: Abandon someone who has no family, take public credit for your good deeds

deities.name: The Offering Plate
deities.edicts: give to those in need, pass on skills and resources to others, contribute your unique talents to society
deities.anathemas: take resources from those who need them, act selfishly, keep knowledge or wealth to yourself

deities.name: The Pale Horse
deities.edicts: Aid and be of service to friends and allies, fulfill your duties, seek vengeance
deities.anathemas: Break an oath, cower from combat, damage or destroy a soul, show mercy to those who betray you

deities.name: The Pandemonia
deities.edicts: disrupt the status quo, never given reasons for your actions, play elaborate tricks on the unsuspecting
deities.anathemas: fall into a set routine, fix something that you have broken

deities.name: The Path of the Heavens
deities.edicts: guide travelers; observe the movements of the moon, planets, and stars
deities.anathemas: mislead a traveler, permanently blind an enemy
deities.source_book: Battle of the Pantheons Winner Announcement
deities.source_text: Battle of the Pantheons Winner Announcement pg. 1

deities.name: The Perplexing Jest
deities.edicts: embarrass and expose hypocrites, humble those in power, play pranks and practical jokes
deities.anathemas: physically harm anyone except your target with your pranks, show remorse for your pranks, take a joke personally

deities.name: The Readied Strike
deities.edicts: encourage unity against common foes, maintain clear lines of communication during warfare, share training and tactics with allies
deities.anathemas: abandon or lie to allies, initiate violence against unidentified persons, refuse to train those seeking to learn

deities.name: The Resplendent Court
deities.edicts: adorn yourself with gems, search for rare clothing and accessories, use your clothing as a means of self-expression
deities.anathemas: dress in plain garments, keep a low profile, wear a uniform

deities.name: The Spirit Wall
deities.edicts: establish and protect your personal territory, punish trespassers, safeguard lawful visitors
deities.anathemas: abandon a guard post, cede territory to an invader

deities.name: The Tides of Chaos
deities.edicts: keep all secrets entrusted to you, sow mistrust, doubt, and conspiracy at every opportunity, take risks
deities.anathemas: aid in the establishment of a government or ruling power, embrace complacency, reveal secrets entrusted to you

deities.name: Thisamet
deities.edicts: Celebrate freely, share prosperity, invite all to your table
deities.anathemas: Ruin a holiday, quarrel at a feast, ignore workers when celebrating

deities.name: Thoth
deities.edicts: Maintain order in society and the multiverse, innovate scientific and magical knowledge, record events
deities.anathemas: Upset stable mechanisms or ecosystems, fail to correct false information

deities.name: Thremyr
deities.edicts: Take any valuables you come across, fiercely defend your treasure, never back down from a fight
deities.anathemas: Show anyone in your command favoritism, give up any of your own treasures, take the Test of the Starstone

deities.name: Thuskchoon
deities.edicts: Devour everything at your disposal, discover hidden knowledge by any means, relish in your desires
deities.anathemas: Fast of your own accord, keep information to yourself, let food rot

deities.name: Titivilus
deities.edicts: Quote people back to themselves, chronicle the times you live in, skew the truth to your purpose
deities.anathemas: Allow a rumor to go unchallenged, destroy records, trust in memory alone

deities.name: Tjasse
deities.edicts: Only ever hunt birds for food, aggressively oppose those who do not have your best interest at heart, protect your children at all cost
deities.anathemas: Harm birds for sport or via negligence, bow down to your oppressor, show mercy to anyone who wronged your child

deities.name: Tlehar
deities.edicts: Give yourself fully to everything you attempt, always maintain hope that tomorrow will be a better day, treasure every gift you are given by those who matter to you
deities.anathemas: Lose your motivation to your regrets, spread despair, treat a loved one poorly

deities.name: Tolte Coatl
deities.edicts: Encourage others to join you on travels, offer to teach those who are genuinely eager to learn, ask others to share their tales
deities.anathemas: Alter the memory of another, deny someone who genuinely wishes to journey with you and would not harm you

deities.name: Torag
deities.edicts: be honorable and forthright, keep your word, respect the forge, serve your people
deities.anathemas: tell lies or cheat someone, intentionally create inferior works, show continued mercy to the enemies of your people when such enemies prove they are undeserving

deities.name: Touch of the Sun
deities.edicts: help others with changes that can improve their lives, mind those that others forget or exclude, offer second chances, stand up for others when they can't stand for themselves
deities.anathemas: challenge the sun, ignore the pleas of others when you are able to help, refuse to give someone else a chance to offer their truth

deities.name: Treasures of the Eternal Delve
deities.edicts: appreciate the beauty of all stones, safely mine for ore, walk barefoot across soil or stones when possible
deities.anathemas: purposefully cause a cave-in, shatter a precious gem

deities.name: Treerazer
deities.edicts: corrupt plant life with evil or fungal influences, slay elves, feast on rotten flesh or fungus
deities.anathemas: grant mercy to elves, plant trees, encourage natural plant growth

deities.name: Trelmarixian
deities.edicts: End all mortal life through wasting consumption and starvation, violently consume matter and souls
deities.anathemas: Kill or remove a parasite or tumor, grow food

deities.name: Tresmalvos
deities.edicts: Chase material wealth, oppress the less fortunate, think only of the current moment
deities.anathemas: Give a gift without expectation of reciprocation, participate in maintaining urban infrastructure

deities.name: Trudd
deities.edicts: Offer your strength to aid others, protect those weaker than you
deities.anathemas: Engage in petty demonstrations of strength, use your strength to take advantage of others

deities.name: Tsukiyo
deities.edicts: provide aid and counsel without judgment to those who seek help, help the dead find their rest, amplify or help speak for the powerless and demonized
deities.anathemas: harm another out of envy, force aid on those who do not want it, inflict harmful mental effects on others as punishment, knowingly hurt the innocent, commit murder

deities.name: Turvu
deities.edicts: travel and explore the world and other planes, seek to understand all sides of a conflict
deities.anathemas: deem anything purely good or evil without first seeking context, remain stagnant in the pursuit of exploration or knowledge

deities.name: Uirch
deities.edicts: Protect the weak and innocent, keep your word, do not throw your life away needlessly
deities.anathemas: Allow tyranny to stand uncontested, put your own safety before those in need of defending, make a promise with the intent to break it

deities.name: Ulon
deities.edicts: Discover and unravel the hidden truths, motivate others to fight and question each other, keep secrets close to the chest
deities.anathemas: Blindly accept what you’re told, share the All-Truth, support or promote unjustified hierarchy

deities.name: Umarik
deities.edicts: Spend time appreciating storms, support your family and close friends, seek places where lightning has struck
deities.anathemas: Take shelter from rain when you are healthy, warn fools about upcoming storms, seek positions of political power

deities.name: Upion and Warrik
deities.edicts: Share stories that glorify your companions, guard your friends' backs, find joy in grim circumstances
deities.anathemas: Betray a lover, flee battle before your companions have a chance to escape

deities.name: Urazra
deities.edicts: Fight anyone who disrespects you, be in the front line of battle, fight to the death
deities.anathemas: Flee from battle, show mercy to your enemy, beg for mercy

deities.name: Urban Prosperity
deities.edicts: celebrate the benefits of urban life, find common ground and establish harmony, protect your city from intrusion or decay, welcome everyone
deities.anathemas: betray the safety of your city, disparage urban life, foment animosity or contention, refuse hospitality or aid, move to the country

deities.name: Urgathoa
deities.edicts: become undead upon death, create or protect the undead, sate your appetites
deities.anathemas: deny your appetites, destroy undead of no harm to you, sacrifice your life

deities.name: Uskyeria
deities.edicts: Preserve and protect natural resources, respect the animals you kill and their spirits, sleep as long as you need to in order to have strength for the day
deities.anathemas: Kill animals for reasons other than self-defense or sustenance, over-hunt the land, prevent others from getting as much sleep as they want

deities.name: Ussharassim
deities.edicts: Patron chroniclers and playwrights, perform dramatic entrances and exits, squeeze your connections for all they’re worth, get the last word
deities.anathemas: Act with modesty or anonymity, perform great deeds without witnesses, let a slight go unrequited

deities.name: Uvuko
deities.edicts: Embrace change and the future, master adversity with flexibility, foster freedom and progress for others
deities.anathemas: Allow yourself and your surroundings to stagnate, crush an egg, use vile or cruel language

deities.name: Valani
deities.edicts: Encourage change for the sake of rebirth, respect the power of the elements, focus on your own self growth
deities.anathemas: Refuse to accept changes in yourself or others, repress you own anger or laughter, use the forces of nature for wanton destruction

deities.name: Vale
deities.edicts: Learn and share and stories of the dead, chronicle languages and cultural traditions, protect knowledge
deities.anathemas: Allow intent to sway your judgment of an action, disrespect the traditions of those around you

deities.name: Valmallos
deities.edicts: Complicate magical techniques, refine arcane fundamentals, teach arcane apprentices to treat magic responsibly
deities.anathemas: Grant magic to those who cannot use it responsibly, cause a magical disaster, ignore magical misconduct

deities.name: Vapula
deities.edicts: Finders keepers, speak with others in their native language, ask more questions than you give answers
deities.anathemas: Work in approximations, renegotiate, fail to uphold your word

deities.name: Varg
deities.edicts: Use tactics rather than relying solely on brute force or overwhelming numbers, use siege engines for combat whenever possible, seek out new technology
deities.anathemas: Pursue technology for non-combat purposes, use machinery and other advanced technology outside of combat, keep advanced weaponry or battle tactics a secret from orcs

deities.name: Vavaalrav
deities.edicts: Create and erect grave markers, lay bodies to rest, protect graveyards and sacred grounds from despoilment and supernatural threats
deities.anathemas: Treat grave sites irreverently, allow others to despoil the dead or rob a grave, create undead, desecrate a corpse

deities.name: Velgaas
deities.edicts: Be sweet with lies and cruel with truths, corrupt the good intentions of others, master the art of manipulation
deities.anathemas: Be obvious with your lies, reveal the truth without benefit

deities.name: Venkelvore
deities.edicts: Gain beauty through pain and effort, keep a meal within arms length, torment those you eat
deities.anathemas: Reject a gift of food, neglect your appearance, starve yourself

deities.name: Verilorn
deities.edicts: Patiently plant your seeds, plan for orderly beauty, watch over your sproutlings
deities.anathemas: Abandon your post, neglect your crops, purposely pervert nature

deities.name: Vermilion Mother
deities.edicts: Indulge in substances derived from psychoactive plants, kill any who would pollute without mercy, protect endangered plants above all else
deities.anathemas: Alter a piece of unspoiled land, purposely destroy plants

deities.name: Vildeis
deities.edicts: Sacrifice yourself in pursuit of good, champion noble causes, scar your body
deities.anathemas: Joke or laugh about injustice, sacrifice others in your place, indulge in luxury

deities.name: Vineshvakhi
deities.edicts: Protect holy people, places, and treasures; sacrifice common comforts and mortal joys for the sake of your charge
deities.anathemas: Abandon your post, fail to protect your charge, willingly suffer corruption over death or grievous harm

deities.name: Vonymos
deities.edicts: Aid the depressed, comfort the grieving, experience and act on intense emotions
deities.anathemas: Attempt to regulate others’ emotions, dismiss or mock a creature’s suffering, surrender without a fight

deities.name: Vorasha
deities.edicts: Lay your enemies low through deceit and subtlety, preserve your patience for longterm goals, venerate all scaled creatures
deities.anathemas: Cultivate or maintain a garden of nonpoisonous plants, intentionally harm a venomous serpent not in self-defense

deities.name: Vudravati
deities.edicts: be generous and forgiving, show respect for all beings and for nature, work to build peaceful communities
deities.anathemas: destroy natural coastlines, sow division through lies or rumors, stoke enmity between rivals

deities.name: Vulot
deities.edicts: Steal another’s identity, never introduce yourself the same way twice, use someone else’s reputation to your advantage
deities.anathemas: Expose your unadorned face, reveal a conspiracy to the public, surrender a criminal to the authorities

deities.name: Wadjet
deities.edicts: Defend your homelands and your people, aid childbirths, grow papyrus, protect sources of clean water
deities.anathemas: Refuse to help a drowning creature, harm a rightful ruler

deities.name: Walkena
deities.edicts: Uphold Mzali’s laws, tend to Walkena and obey his instructions, oppose exploitation of the Mwangi Expanse
deities.anathemas: Consort or trade with non-Mwangi peoples, defy Walkena’s orders

deities.name: Wards of the Pharaoh
deities.edicts: Use magic to defend yourself and your allies, maintain magical wards, seek greater magical knowledge
deities.anathemas: Destroy wards that are actively protecting innocents, refuse to use your magic to help those in need who ask you

deities.name: Waves of the Boundless Sea
deities.edicts: allow the currents of life take you where they may, travel the expansive seas, wash away dust and dirt with seawater
deities.anathemas: allow a creature to drown when you could save them, toss detritus into the oceans

deities.name: Weight of the World
deities.edicts: meditate on the cycle of nature, teach others to be prepared for storms and other natural hazards, wield the elements as weapons
deities.anathemas: destroy nature without an immediate need, pass up an opportunity to watch storms and other natural phenomena

deities.name: Wheels of Innovation
deities.edicts: devise solutions to problems through the creative use of items rather than magic or brute strength, encourage tinkering and experimentation, refuse to abandon a project at the first sign of failure
deities.anathemas: deprive others of their innovations unduly, stifle imagination

deities.name: Whispering Way
deities.edicts: seek the perfection of undeath, cleanse the world of the living, master the magical secrets of life and death
deities.anathemas: speak the secrets of the Whispering Way above a whisper, write down the philosophies of the Whispering Way, act irrationally due to concerns for the living

deities.name: Winlas
deities.edicts: Serve leaders of ceremonies, craft ceremonial arms and armor, lead a congregation
deities.anathemas: Deride sacred ceremonies, carelessly or lazily perform rituals, destroy ceremonial objects

deities.name: Wulgren
deities.edicts: Create the weapons you intend to wield with your own two hands, innovate efficient ways to defeat your enemies
deities.anathemas: Wield a weapon forged by another except in direst circumstance, yield ground to an enemy while victory is still possible

deities.name: Xhamen-Dor
deities.edicts: Spread fungal growths, subtly infect others with knowledge of Xhamen-Dor
deities.anathemas: Outer Gods and Great Old Ones

deities.name: Xiquiripat
deities.edicts: Never show mercy, pursue your enemies to their end or yours, teach all to fear you
deities.anathemas: Accept surrender from an enemy, show cowardice or weakness

deities.name: Xoveron
deities.edicts: Acquire and consume more resources than you need, embrace stillness, ensure ruins remain in their dilapidated state
deities.anathemas: Allow a structure to be rebuilt or maintained, practice moderation in consumption

deities.name: Xsistaid
deities.edicts: Feed on others without guilt, rob others of strength to embolden yourself, wield your weakness as your weapon
deities.anathemas: Fail to take advantage of someone’s kindness, take only what you need when you could get away with more

deities.name: Yaezhing
deities.edicts: commit assassinations for hire, strike unseen, carry out punishment for convicted criminals
deities.anathemas: show mercy to a target, take credit for your assassinations, refuse to punish a lawfully convicted criminal, slaughter indiscriminately

deities.name: Yamasoth
deities.edicts: Embrace your worst aspects, perform dangerous experiments on yourself and others, whisper foul secrets in the ears of leaders
deities.anathemas: Avoid new experiences, try to steer a community away from a deadly fate

deities.name: Yamatsumi
deities.edicts: strive to be self-sufficient, respect nature, test yourself against the elements
deities.anathemas: become reliant on civilization, destroy something without creating or growing something in its place

deities.name: Ydajisk
deities.edicts: Create using words, chronicle languages and prevent them from dying, help language evolve
deities.anathemas: Ban or discourage a language, explain a secret language or slang to outsiders, destroy literary works

deities.name: Ydersius
deities.edicts: seek to return Ydersius to life, fulfill your passions, conquer your foes with no mercy, achieve glory for serpentkind
deities.anathemas: put the needs of others above those of serpentfolk, aid the spawn of Azlant

deities.name: Yelayne
deities.edicts: Develop a signature style, express yourself through craft, provide necessities and protection for those in need
deities.anathemas: Steal another artisan’s design, participate in an arranged marriage

deities.name: Yhidothrus
deities.edicts: Advance the destruction of all things, extend your life by any means necessary, sow discord among civilizations
deities.anathemas: Act rashly without careful planning, promote the creation of a place, thing, or idea

deities.name: Yig
deities.edicts: Feast in the autumn, perform seasonal rituals, protect your home from trespassers
deities.anathemas: fang or Katar
devotee_benefits: Outer Gods and Great Old Ones

deities.name: Ylimancha
deities.edicts: Teach sustainable fishing, swim in saltwater, fly
deities.anathemas: Imprison birds or clip their wings, poison coastal waters, overfish, aid Pazuzu or his minions

deities.name: Yluma
deities.edicts: celebrate your victories and losses, embrace your unique strengths and innate gifts, nurture the flame of acceptance and knowledge in others.
deities.anathemas: intentionally mislead people away from understanding themselves, repeatedly deny who you are, treat your gifts as a curse

deities.name: Ymeri
deities.edicts: Be passionate and quick of wit, destroy your foes with fire, inspire your inferiors with zeal and strategy
deities.anathemas: Allow yourself to stagnate or lose motivation, extinguish destructive blazes

deities.name: Yog-Sothoth
deities.edicts: Gather knowledge of gates through space and time, curse or mutate unborn children
deities.anathemas: Outer Gods and Great Old Ones

deities.name: Yrmidar
deities.edicts: Help your fellow neighbor, properly maintain ships and other sailing vessels you own, contribute to your community
deities.anathemas: Be too proud to give or ask for help, intentionally provoke wild animals, choose yourself over your community

deities.name: Ytildos
deities.edicts: Capitalize on others' misfortune for profit, subtly ruin navigation tools, set traps
deities.anathemas: Despoil a coastal ecosystem, give away a treasure map for free

deities.name: Yuelral
deities.edicts: Practice herbalism, use and enchant gems, encourage and teach magicians and jewelers, preserve elven magic and knowledge
deities.anathemas: Cut a gem for aesthetic purposes, defile nature, allow the irresponsible use of magic

deities.name: Zarongel
deities.edicts: Learn mounted combat, perform arson, hunt and kill dogs
deities.anathemas: Befriend or spare a dog

deities.name: Zeaki
deities.edicts: Inflict poetic justice upon those who wrong you, assist others' personal transformations, learn esoteric secrets that others might value
deities.anathemas: Become complacent with your current self, offer aid to a stranger without exacting some price (even if the cost isn't obvious)

deities.name: Zelishkar
deities.edicts: Burn the remains of your defeated enemies and closest allies, destroy the accomplishments of your so-called betters, wield flame as a tool and a weapon
deities.anathemas: Accept the motivations of others at face value, extinguish a raging fire

deities.name: Zevgavizeb
deities.edicts: Slaughter the cowardly, subjugate the weak
deities.anathemas: Favor stealth over shows of strength, eat cooked meat

deities.name: Zibik
deities.edicts: foster natural cycles of decay and regrowth, eradicate invasive creatures, treat unnatural diseases
deities.anathemas: ignore mistreatment of the environment, delay natural death, cause imbalance in an ecosystem

deities.name: Zipacna
deities.edicts: Do only what you must, eat your fill, overpower anyone who challenges you
deities.anathemas: Serve others, allow laziness to create more work for yourself, refuse an indulgence

deities.name: Zjar-Tovan
deities.edicts: aid those who cannot freely live as they would choose to, do not allow your purpose to be overly guided by another’s will, inspire others with your personal convictions
deities.anathemas: belittle an ally (or someone you respect) when they express their beliefs, serve a cause you do not truly believe in

deities.name: Zogmugot
deities.edicts: Ply the sea, work fervently and without help, use what is given to you
deities.anathemas: Unnecessarily seek divine attention or guidance, disrespect the water, bury a piece of sea glass

deities.name: Zohls
deities.edicts: Solve logic puzzles, investigate crimes, devise new solutions from research
deities.anathemas: Make judgments without evidence, contaminate evidence, obstruct truths

deities.name: Zon-Kuthon
deities.edicts: bring pain to the world, mutilate your body
deities.anathemas: create permanent or long-lasting sources of light, provide comfort to those who suffer

deities.name: Zugero
deities.edicts: Fight today so you can rest tomorrow, work smart instead of hard, slack off and get away with it
deities.anathemas: Launch yourself thoughtlessly into work or combat

deities.name: Zura
deities.edicts: Drink blood, seek vampirism, cause bleed damage
deities.anathemas: Expose vampires, heal a bloody wound without drinking blood from it first

deities.name: Zursvaater
deities.edicts: Conquer the weak, devise plans ahead of anticipated battles, only bring wellmaintained weapons to fights
deities.anathemas: Engage in violent infighting, claim anyone but Zursvaater gives you your strength, disrespect a skilled smith

deities.name: Zyphus
deities.edicts: encourage deadly accidents by performing acts of sabotage or through faulty design, seek out instances of tragedy and foster ill will in the surviving victims, spread the belief that life is pointless since it all ends in the pain of death
deities.anathemas: engineer the same accident twice in a row, knowingly provide aid to a Pharasmin, spread hope or optimism (particularly in the face of a tragedy)
